title: Under AIADMK Rule There Is Darkness, Deception, Everything!
translatorNote: This translation is intended to carry Kalaignar's voice into clear, contemporary English rather than recast the letter as literary or academic prose. It preserves the source's argument, political directness, rhetorical questions, repetition, irony, factual detail, and paragraph order. Names, dates, figures, quotations, and intentional English expressions are retained. Where Tamil idiom cannot be reproduced literally without sounding unnatural, the English follows its sense and rhetorical force without adding claims absent from the source. The Tamil below is a source-checked reading copy: the opening salutation omitted by OCR has been restored; quoted claims and comparative statistics have been retained; and clear OCR character, numeral and word-break errors have been repaired. The canonical source file remains unchanged and authoritative. `Udanpirappē` is retained in Tamil transliteration rather than flattened into “brother,” “sister,” or “comrade.” Literally evoking “one born alongside me,” Kalaignar uses it as a distinctive address of shared identity, equality, affection, and solidarity within the movement.
paragraphs: On the twenty-third, Chief Minister O. Panneerselvam replied to the debate on the Governor's Address delivered on 17 February 2015. Rather than answer opposition speeches, he apparently regarded it as his principal duty to offer Assembly birthday greetings to his party General Secretary Jayalalithaa, the former Chief Minister removed from office after a Bengaluru Special Court sentenced her to four years' imprisonment and fined her Rs 100 crore.

In the House he said, “Amma will certainly sit upon the throne again.” Yet his two-page advertisement in the party newspaper says only: “Amma, who turns strength into a weapon and continues to govern, your sixty-seventh birthday is Tamil Nadu's festival. I pray to God that you live many years in fame.” It omits “will sit upon the throne again,” revealing his deepest inner wish. Carefully, he issued even that advertisement only as party Treasurer, not as Chief Minister. Perhaps, since it says she “continues to govern,” he thought he should not call himself Chief Minister!

Should the Assembly offer birthday greetings to someone convicted by a court? This Government throws every convention aside and does whatever it pleases. It calls the Leader of the Opposition a “citizen” in the House; when his party rises to object, it denies democratic permission and has them removed by marshals. Then it admits one marshal to hospital, obtains a complaint from him and tries to arrest opposition MLAs.

Journalists must tell the people whether any of this is proper. Whether one alleged act can attract multiple proceedings by the Privileges Committee and police is a question of natural justice requiring deep examination.

Commenting on the Governor's Address, I said it was an advertisement for what the Government claimed to have done last year and announced no constructive plan for the coming year. The Chief Minister's reply strengthens that criticism. It too discussed only the past and said nothing about what the Government will do this year.

His speech sounded like a Finance Minister replying to a general Budget debate. Perhaps, having served as Finance Minister, he still has not remembered that he is now Chief Minister. If asked, he may explain: “Only if we intended to do something could we mention it in this year's Governor's Address. With our financial resources exhausted and no schemes available, how could we say anything? Had we intended to plan, would we not have convened the annual District Collectors' conference at the end of last year? If I announce a plan on my own, must I not also remain in this office afterwards?”

I asked whether a Governor's Address should praise a convicted person. It did not even call her former Chief Minister Jayalalithaa, merely “Selvi J. Jayalalithaa.” We expected Panneerselvam to explain. He never opened his mouth.

I raised Classical Tamil, the failure to give Tamil due importance and the introduction of English-medium instruction in Government schools. His reply said nothing. I asked: “You welcome the Union Government's replacement of the Planning Commission. What, then, is the fate of Tamil Nadu's State Planning Commission?” No answer.

I asked about reports that ninety lakh people had still not received the AIADMK's free mixers, grinders and fans and that distributed goats and cattle were being sold in markets. The Chief Minister answered as though to say, “What can I do?” by saying nothing at all.

I asked why highway projects were being handed to private parties. I asked why the global investors' conference announced for last October never occurred and had now been shifted to May. Panneerselvam's attitude was: “Who are you to ask and who am I to answer? I am Amma's foremost disciple.”

DMK speakers younger brothers I. Periyasamy and S.S. Sivasankar, and members of other opposition parties, raised issue after issue. Did Panneerselvam answer any? No. He merely reread his earlier statement on power schemes.

I asked what became of the Ennore Replacement Thermal Project and Uppur Thermal Project. He replied that action had been taken to obtain electricity from them. What became of North Chennai Stage III, the Udangudi expansion and the replacement for the Thoothukudi Thermal Project? He said steps were being taken to secure environmental clearance.
Where is the Cheyyur Thermal Power Station? His answer: land acquisition has been completed.

On 25 April 2013, Jayalalithaa announced under Rule 110 the Rs 7,000-crore, 2,000-MW Sillahalla pumped-storage project; Rs 8,000 crore for generating stations and transmission corridors; Rs 500 crore for twenty thousand new transformers; and Rs 850 crore for fifteen thousand kilometres of power lines. These schemes, totalling Rs 16,350 crore, would rapidly make Tamil Nadu power-surplus and guarantee uninterrupted, stable electricity. I asked what became of them.

Do you know the Chief Minister's answer yesterday? “Survey work for the 2,000-MW project is proceeding vigorously.” What does “rapidly” mean under this Government? A week? A month? A year? Or “never; it will not happen at all”?

Panneerselvam also claimed his Government had created a path to receive 22,440 MW. If that is true, why has the Tamil Nadu Generation and Distribution Corporation contracted to buy 3,330 MW under long-term agreements and 500 MW under medium-term procurement? Does the Government have a duty to explain or not?

The Chief Minister said food-grain production was 10.152 million tonnes in 2011-12 and 11.002 million tonnes in 2013-14. Why did it decline in 2012-13? Was the AIADMK not in office then too? With *kuruvai* cultivation failing in the delta for three successive years, farmers themselves doubt these production figures.

He boasts that Rs 1,328.50 crore in drought relief was paid to 2,089,554 farmers in 2012-13. Did every affected farmer receive complete and proper relief? Has he forgotten that the DMK promised before taking office to waive approximately Rs 7,000 crore in cooperative agricultural loans and fulfilled it, benefiting 2,240,739 farming families, many of them AIADMK supporters?

The Chief Minister pointed to an increase in the milk procurement price to Rs 28 a litre. Should he not also mention the sale price? Immediately after taking office in 2011, the AIADMK raised the DMK-era price of Rs 17.75 to Rs 24, an increase of Rs 6.25. In October 2014, it added another Rs 10, taking the price to Rs 34. Can Panneerselvam hide the achievement of nearly doubling the sale price?

He said nothing of corruption in Aavin. One Minister was sent home and a ruling-party member arrested. If there is no weight in one's lap, should he not have honestly placed those details before the House?

On animal husbandry, Panneerselvam said 585 sub-centres were upgraded to rural veterinary dispensaries and twenty new dispensaries established under the AIADMK. Under the DMK in 2007-08, 888 veterinary sub-centres were upgraded. Fifty-one new dispensaries, thirty new sub-centres and fifty-five mobile units were opened for Rs 218.24 lakh. I also remind him that Asia's first veterinary university was created under a DMK Government.

Panneerselvam said the AIADMK doubled assistance for marine fishing families during the ban season from Rs 1,000 to Rs 2,000. It was the DMK Government that first raised it from Rs 500 to Rs 800 and then doubled it to Rs 1,000. Only afterwards did the AIADMK double it again.

Under the National Fishermen Savings-cum-Relief Scheme funded by the Union and State Governments, the DMK paid Rs 88,57,01,029 to 180,000 fishermen annually between May 2006 and January 2011. Many other fishermen's welfare schemes were also implemented.

Panneerselvam said the Union Government had been asked to arrange talks between Tamil Nadu and Sri Lankan fishermen in Chennai on 5 March 2015. I remind him that the talks will not occur on the fifth; they have been postponed.

On school education, he read figures saying the AIADMK opened 182 primary schools, upgraded 107 primary schools to middle schools, 810 middle schools to high schools and 400 high schools to higher-secondary schools.

Under the DMK from 2006 to 2010-11, 569 new primary schools were opened, 2,626 primary schools became middle schools, 645 middle schools became high schools, and 570 high schools became higher-secondary schools. I also note that the details Panneerselvam read are ordinarily supplied by individual Ministers during departmental grant debates and in policy notes. He must at least now free himself from the role of a Minister and try to answer as Chief Minister.
He attempted some reply on law and order. I need not answer it. Newspapers daily publish murders, robberies, waylaying, chain-snatching and fraud, and Tamil Nadu's people read and know them.

Conveniently hiding all that, Panneerselvam said: “Unlike the former minority Government, there is no darkness, no theft, no deception, no fabrication.” How long would it take to answer in his own style: “Under convicted Jayalalithaa's Government, there is darkness, theft, deception and fabrication”?
With affection,
M.K.
26 February 2015
